macro_events.event_summary: Putin visits Beijing days after Trump's own China summit, highlighting Beijing's geopolitical balancing act between Washington and Moscow amid the ongoing Ukraine war and energy market tensions.
primary_drivers: Geopolitical hedging premium in energy / defense
Signal of continued Russia-China economic axis (sanctions evasion, energy flows)
Uncertainty over US-China detente durability following Trump visit

beneficiaries.rationale: Prolonged Russia-China alignment hardens the case for sustained US/NATO defense budgets; Lockheed is the most direct large-cap beneficiary via F-35, PAC-3, and HIMARS demand from European replenishment.
Patriot/Stinger/AMRAAM backlog tied directly to Ukraine support and European air defense buildout; visible China-Russia coordination keeps Congressional aid flowing.
B-21 and strategic deterrent programs gain political tailwind when China is seen as actively aligning with a nuclear adversary; pure-play on great-power competition budgets.

If summit signals continued Russian energy displacement from Western markets, US baseload power exporters and LNG-adjacent generators benefit; Constellation is the cleanest US nuclear pure-play with rising power prices.
Cheniere benefits from any narrative that European/Asian buyers diversify away from Russian gas; a visible Putin-Xi axis reinforces the structural US LNG export thesis.

losers.rationale: Boeing has been the marquee beneficiary of Trump's China visit (rumored large order book); a Putin visit immediately after muddies the goodwill narrative and reintroduces risk to any pending China commercial aircraft deal.

Tesla's Shanghai gigafactory and China sales (~20% of revenue) make it the most rate-sensitive megacap to US-China relations cooling; a visible Russia tilt by Beijing raises tail risk of renewed friction post-Trump trip.
Heavy China handset OEM revenue exposure (~60%); any signal that Beijing is hedging back toward Moscow undermines the post-summit thaw narrative that supported semis with China exposure.
Macau-dependent name that rallies on US-China detente headlines; Putin visit framing as 'test of China's balancing act' caps the post-Trump-summit bounce.

headlines: Putin heads to Beijing days after Trump in test of China's balancing act

macro_events.event_summary: Crude oil prices fell after Trump postponed a potential strike on Iran, easing fears of Middle East supply disruption and Strait of Hormuz risk premium.
primary_drivers: Removal of geopolitical risk premium from crude prices
Lower input/fuel costs for energy consumers
Compressed margins for upstream oil producers

beneficiaries.rationale: Jet fuel is ~25-30% of operating costs for legacy carriers; lower crude flows directly to margin expansion. Delta has limited fuel hedging vs peers, so spot price drops hit P&L faster.
Same jet-fuel cost sensitivity as DAL; United is similarly under-hedged and trades as a high-beta proxy for crude declines.
Southwest historically hedges fuel but exposure to spot remains material; airline group rotates higher on crude selloffs regardless.
Fuel is a major line item for the express/ground network; lower diesel and jet fuel reduce surcharges-passthrough lag and improve near-term margin.
Naphtha/ethane feedstock costs track crude; lower oil widens petrochemical spreads for ethylene/polyethylene producers.

losers.rationale: Integrated major with heavy upstream exposure; lower Brent/WTI directly compresses E&P segment earnings. Largest weight in energy ETFs so absorbs sector outflows.
Pure-play US shale producer with high oil-price beta and leveraged balance sheet; among the most sensitive S&P energy names to crude moves.
Permian pure-play upstream; earnings and FCF essentially a linear function of WTI, so risk-premium unwind hits valuation directly.
Oilfield services demand and pricing power tied to E&P capex, which softens when crude falls and operators trim drilling budgets.
International services exposure including Middle East; lower crude + reduced geopolitical urgency reduces near-term activity premium.
Cheniere benefits from elevated geopolitical bid for US LNG cargoes; de-escalation narrows TTF/JKM spreads and pressures spot cargo economics.

headlines: Oil prices fall as Trump postpones Iran strike, easing supply disruption fears
